methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $810M - $4.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.7x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
DNA Script is a Series C-stage biotech company in the Biotech & Health sector, having raised $200M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 4 same-sector, same-stage private companies (Ultima Genomics, Insitro, Colossal Biosciences, New Limit) with valuations ranging $810M–$4.0B, plus 3 public biotech comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two quantitative methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a baseline proxy of $200M and applying a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier for Series C advancement, implies a valuation of $340M. Peer-median analysis across the four identified private comparables yields a median valuation of $1.9B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to the absence of target revenue data, which limits P/S multiple methods. The peer range ($810M–$4.0B) reflects meaningful dispersion, driven primarily by differences in commercial traction and platform maturity within the sector.

Triangulation of the two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $289M (low) to $2.2B (high), with a median estimate of $1.1B. The wide spread reflects tension between DNA Script's early-stage capital efficiency metrics and comparable-company valuations. Key uncertainties include unreported revenue or commercial milestones, which would anchor the high end of the range, and the relative technical differentiation of the platform against better-capitalized peers like Ultima Genomics.
